title: ingest_content
description: Begin ingestion of raw user content. Call this as the first step of the ingest loop, before deciding how to store it. Returns a structured NoteSpec candidate (parsed title, type guess, summary, tags, body markdown). Pure parsing only; performs no writes and no retrieval. Follow up with retrieve_similar_notes to dedup-check before save_note.

content.description: The raw user content to ingest. Pasted text, transcript output, OCR output, page text, etc. Pass it verbatim — do not pre-summarize. Must be non-empty; if you have nothing to ingest, do not call this tool.

source.description: Where the content came from. Use 'chat-paste' for arbitrary text the user pasted into chat, 'manual' when the user is dictating a note, and the file-type-specific values when the upload was a file of that kind. This value is propagated into the saved note's frontmatter.source so retrieval can filter by origin.

instructions.description: Optional free-text instruction the user gave alongside the content (e.g. 'split into per-attendee notes', 'tag as q2-planning'). Pass null when the user gave no instruction. Do not invent instructions; the parser respects this field literally.
